item.title: Tesla just increased its spending plan to $25B — here’s where the money is going
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Tesla's planned capex for 2026 is three times higher than what the company has historically spent. Its CFO said, as a result, Tesla will have a negative free cash flow the rest of the year.

item.title: Google updates Workspace to make AI your new office intern
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Google has introduced a host of new automated functions into Workspace, all of which are driven by Workspace Intelligence, its new AI system.

item.title: Hands on with X’s new AI-powered custom feeds
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X's AI-powered custom timelines are replacing Communities, with Grok-curated feeds...and new ad slots.

item.title: How SpaceX preempted a $2B fundraise with a $60B buyout offer
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round this week but chose to halt discussions after SpaceX offered a $10 billion "collaboration fee" and a path to a $60 billion acquisition.

item.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

item.title: Google turns Chrome into an AI co-worker for the workplace
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google brings Gemini-powered "auto browse" capabilities to Chrome for enterprise users, letting workers automate tasks like research, data entry, and more.

item.title: Google makes an interesting choice with its new agent-building tool for enterprises
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform takes an interesting approach: It is geared for IT and technical users.

item.title: AI Overviews are coming to your Gmail at work
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: The AI Overviews will offer instant summaries pulled from across multiple emails.

item.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Infosys said the integration will be used to help its clients modernize software development, automate workflows, and deploy AI systems, initially focusing software engineering, legacy modernization, and DevOps.

item.title: AI is spitting out more potential drugs than ever. This startup wants to figure out which ones matter.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: 10x Science has raised a $4.8 million seed round to help pharmaceutical researchers understand complex molecules.

item.title: Exclusive: Google deepens Thinking Machines Lab ties with new multibillion-dollar deal
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab has signed a multibillion-dollar deal with Google Cloud for AI infrastructure powered by Nvidia's latest GB300 chips, TechCrunch has exclusively learned.

item.title: Google Maps is about to get a big dose of AI
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The new features, announced at Cloud Next in Las Vegas this week, add generative AI capabilities to Google's mapping platform, giving it enhanced visual and data analytics powers.

item.title: The most interesting startups showcased at Google Cloud Next 2026
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Google wants AI startups on its cloud and has showcased a long list of them at its annual conference.

item.title: Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

item.title: Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

item.title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60B
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the same companies now competing directly with Cursor for the developer market.

item.title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

item.title: AI research lab NeoCognition lands $40M seed to build agents that learn like humans
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Founded by an OSU researcher, the startup is developing AI agents that can become experts in any domain.

item.title: ChatGPT’s new Images 2.0 model is surprisingly good at generating text
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0, the newest image-generation model from OpenAI, shows just how much AI capabilities have evolved over the last few years.

item.title: Sam Altman throws shade at Anthropic’s cyber model, Mythos: ‘fear-based marketing’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: This week, during a podcast appearance, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman called out his competitor's new cybersecurity model, noting that the company was using fear to make its product sound more impressive than it actually is.
